conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Are you currently responsible for Alex - meaning, does Alex normally live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. He lives with his mum.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I understand Alex lives with his mum.

Do you contribute towards Alex's care in any way, such as paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be worth towards his upkeep each week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep. I help out with things like clothes, school dinners, and other bits and pieces regularly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help assess this properly, I need to understand a bit more about the arrangement.

Is Alex's mum currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just to clarify the living arrangements:

Does Alex live with his mum full-time, or does he spend time living with you as well (for example, does he stay with you on certain days of the week or alternate weeks)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he lives with his mum full-time. I don't have him staying with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one more important question:

Are you or have you ever been a partner (married or living together) with Alex's mum?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she's my ex-partner. We're not together anymore.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. I have one final question to complete the assessment:

Do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

To be eligible for Child Benefit, the child must normally live with you. While Alex is within the eligible age range (under 16) and you live in the UK, Alex lives with his mum full-time and not with you.

Although you do contribute financially towards Alex's upkeep by helping with clothes, school dinners, and other expenses, this doesn't meet the key requirement that the child must normally live with the person claiming.

Generally, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and this should be the person the child normally lives with. In this case, that would be Alex's mum. She would be the eligible person to claim Child Benefit for Alex if she chooses to do so.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: According to UK Child Benefit rules, to be eligible you must be responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training) AND the child must normally live with you. While Alex is 8 years old (meeting the age requirement) and the claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with his mum full-time and does not live with the claimant. Although the claimant contributes towards Alex's upkeep with clothes, school dinners and other items, this does not satisfy the requirement that the child must normally live with them. The general rule is that only one person can get Child Benefit for a child, and this should be the person the child normally lives with. Since Alex lives with his mum full-time, the mum would be the person eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex, not the claimant.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because Alex does not normally live with them. The fundamental requirement for Child Benefit is that the child must normally live with the person claiming. While the claimant contributes financially to Alex's upkeep, Alex lives full-time with his mum, making her the eligible person to claim Child Benefit.
